title: leptospermum polygalifolium var. rotundifolium
intro: Leptospermum polygalifolium var. rotundifolium, also known as the rounded-leaf tea tree, is a variant of the Leptospermum polygalifolium plant, which belongs to the myrtle family (Myrtaceae) and is native to Australia. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in traditional medicine, possessing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Leptospermum polygalifolium var. rotundifolium include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
